title: podman build/run defaults to the wrong platform on macOS with qemu-user-static installed which is very slow
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I've been using `podman` on macOS x64 to do cross-platforms builds like so:

1. `podman machine ssh "sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static; sudo systemctl reboot"`
2. `podman build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64,linux/ppc64le,linux/s390x [...]`

This all works great (thank you!); however, ever since I've been doing this, when I just want to do a normal `podman build` or `podman run`, it is defaulting to a non-native platform which is incredibly slow:

```
$ podman run --rm -it fedora uname -m
aarch64
```

`podman run` and `podman build` should default to the platform of the `podman machine`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine ssh "sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static; sudo systemctl reboot"`
2. `podman run --rm -it fedora uname -m`

**Describe the results you received:**

Wrong default architecture:

```
$ podman run --rm -it fedora uname -m
aarch64
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Default architecture should match my `podman machine` and host system:

```
$ podman machine ssh uname -m
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:60221' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
x86_64
$ uname -m
x86_64
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The workaround is to explicitly specify `--platform linux/amd64`

**Output of `podman version`:**

<details><summary>podman version</summary>

```
$ podman version

Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3

Built:        Wed Jun 22 11:17:44 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

</details>

comments.body: @flouthoc, I think you fixed that recently, did you?

> @flouthoc, I think you fixed that recently, did you?

I am unable to recall but afaics it already has the image from last built architecture and storage is still populated with that. @kgibm does it work if you clean all images on local `podman rmi --all -f` and perform `podman build` again ?

@flouthoc Yes, that workaround was successful. Is this expected behavior that it uses the last built architecture?

```
$ podman machine init test
$ podman system connection default test
$ podman machine ssh "sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static; sudo systemctl reboot"
$ podman run --platform linux/arm64 --rm -it fedora uname -m
aarch64
$ podman run --rm -it fedora uname -m
aarch64
$ podman rmi --all -f
$ podman run --rm -it fedora uname -m
x86_64
```

Hi @kgibm , As of now this is expected behavior but we are discussing a solution here https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15300 so closing this as duplicate. Could you please track https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15300.

Thanks
